papers.title: Approximation Theory of Laplacian-Based Neural Operators for Reaction-Diffusion System
papers.summary: Neural operators provide a framework for learning solution operators of partial differential equations (PDEs), enabling efficient surrogate modeling for complex systems. While universal approximation results are now well understood, approximation analysis specific to nonlinear reaction-diffusion systems remains limited. In this paper, we study neural operators applied to the solution mapping from initial conditions to time-dependent solutions of a generalized Gierer-Meinhardt reaction-diffusion system, a prototypical model of nonlinear pattern formation. Our main results establish explicit approximation error bounds in terms of network depth, width, and spectral rank by exploiting the Laplacian spectral representation of the Green's function underlying the PDE. We show that the required parameter complexity grows at most polynomially with respect to the target accuracy, demonstrating that Laplacian eigenfunction-based neural operator architectures alleviate the curse of parametric complexity encountered in generic operator learning. Numerical experiments on the Gierer-Meinhardt system support the theoretical findings.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了基于拉普拉斯算子的神经算子对广义Gierer-Meinhardt反应扩散系统的逼近理论，建立了显式的逼近误差界，并证明参数复杂度随目标精度多项式增长，避免了参数复杂性灾难。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子的通用逼近结果已较完善，但针对非线性反应扩散系统的逼近分析仍有限，需要专门的理论保证。
ai.method: 利用反应扩散系统格林函数的拉普拉斯谱表示，分析神经算子在网络深度、宽度和谱秩方面的逼近误差界。
ai.result: 建立了显式逼近误差界，表明所需参数复杂度随目标精度多项式增长，验证了拉普拉斯特征函数架构能缓解参数复杂性灾难。
ai.help_to_user: 为设计高效、可扩展的反应扩散系统神经算子代理模型提供了理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 结合自适应谱截断与多尺度特征，进一步提升神经算子的逼近效率。
idea_spark.risk: 理论分析依赖于格林函数的谱表示，对于非自伴或非线性算子可能不直接适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受格林函数谱分解启发，可探索其他积分方程核的谱表示在神经算子中的应用。

en.tldr: This paper studies the approximation theory of Laplacian-based neural operators for the generalized Gierer-Meinhardt reaction-diffusion system, establishing explicit error bounds and showing polynomial parameter complexity growth with target accuracy, thus avoiding the curse of parametric complexity.
en.motivation: While universal approximation results for neural operators are well understood, approximation analysis specific to nonlinear reaction-diffusion systems remains limited, requiring dedicated theoretical guarantees.
en.method: Exploiting the Laplacian spectral representation of the Green's function underlying the PDE, the authors analyze approximation error bounds in terms of network depth, width, and spectral rank.
en.result: Explicit approximation error bounds are established, showing that required parameter complexity grows at most polynomially with target accuracy, confirming that Laplacian eigenfunction-based architectures alleviate the curse of parametric complexity.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical guidance for designing efficient and scalable neural operator surrogates for reaction-diffusion systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine adaptive spectral truncation with multi-scale features to further improve approximation efficiency of neural operators.
idea_spark.risk: The theoretical analysis relies on the spectral representation of the Green's function, which may not directly apply to non-self-adjoint or nonlinear operators.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the spectral decomposition of Green's functions, one could explore spectral representations of other integral equation kernels in neural operators.

papers.title: Compositional Neural Operators for Multi-Dimensional Fluid Dynamics
papers.summary: Partial differential equations (PDEs) govern diverse physical phenomena, yet high-fidelity numerical solutions are computationally expensive and Machine Learning approaches lack generalization. While Scientific Foundation Models (SFMs) aim to provide universal surrogates, typical encoding-decoding approaches suffer from high pretraining costs and limited interpretability. In this paper, we propose Compositional Neural Operators (CompNO) for 2D systems, a framework that decomposes complex PDEs into a library of Foundation Blocks. Each block is a specialized Neural Operator pretrained on elementary physics. This modular library contains convection, diffusion, and nonlinear convection blocks as well as a Poisson Solver, enabling the framework to address the pressure-velocity coupling. These experts are assembled via an Adaptation Block featuring an Aggregator. This aggregator learns nonlinear interactions by minimizing data loss and physics-based residuals driven from governing equations. The proposed approach has been evaluated on the Convection-Diffusion equation, the Burgers' equation, and the Incompressible Navier-Stokes equation. Our results demonstrate that learning from elementary operators significantly improves adaptability, enhances model interpretability and facilitates the reuse of pretrained blocks when adapting to new physical systems.

ai.tldr: 提出组合神经算子框架，将复杂PDE分解为基础模块库，通过聚合器组合专家模块，提升泛化性和可解释性。
ai.motivation: 高保真数值解计算昂贵，机器学习方法泛化差，现有科学基础模型预训练成本高且可解释性有限。
ai.method: 构建包含对流、扩散、非线性对流和泊松求解器的模块库，通过适应块中的聚合器最小化数据损失和物理残差来组合模块。
ai.result: 在对流扩散、Burgers和不可压缩Navier-Stokes方程上验证，表明学习基本算子显著提高适应性、可解释性和模块复用性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种模块化、可解释的神经算子框架，适用于多维度流体动力学问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂物理系统分解为基本物理模块，通过可学习的聚合器组合，实现零样本或小样本适应。
idea_spark.risk: 模块库的完备性和聚合器的泛化能力可能受限，复杂耦合系统需更多专家模块。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受组合式科学基础模型启发，将物理先验嵌入模块化架构。

en.tldr: Proposes Compositional Neural Operators (CompNO) that decompose complex PDEs into a library of Foundation Blocks, assembled via an Adaptation Block with an Aggregator, improving adaptability and interpretability.
en.motivation: High-fidelity numerical solutions are expensive, ML approaches lack generalization, and existing SFMs have high pretraining costs and limited interpretability.
en.method: Builds a library of specialized Neural Operators (convection, diffusion, nonlinear convection, Poisson solver) and combines them via an Aggregator that minimizes data loss and physics-based residuals.
en.result: Evaluated on Convection-Diffusion, Burgers', and Incompressible Navier-Stokes equations; shows significant improvement in adaptability, interpretability, and reuse of pretrained blocks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a modular and interpretable neural operator framework for multi-dimensional fluid dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose complex physical systems into elementary physics blocks and learn to combine them for zero-shot or few-shot adaptation.
idea_spark.risk: Completeness of the block library and generalization of the aggregator may be limited; complex couplings may require more expert blocks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by compositional scientific foundation models, embedding physical priors into a modular architecture.

papers.title: Acceleration of horizontal numerical advection for atmospheric modeling through surrogate modeling with temporal coarse-graining
papers.summary: Machine-learned surrogate modeling of advection may accelerate geoscientific models, but existing approaches have either achieved limited speedup or have sacrificed spatial resolution compared to the model they are trained to emulate. We developed a machine-learned solver that speeds up advection simulations without sacrificing spatial resolution through the use of temporal coarse-graining, where the model is trained to take larger integration steps than dictated by the Courant-Friedrich-Lewy (CFL) condition. Our solver framework includes a convolutional neural network that takes concentrations and CFL numbers as inputs and outputs mass flux. Our solvers emulate 10-day ground-level horizontal advection simulations with r$^2$ values against the baseline ranging from 0.60--0.98 with temporal coarsening factors of 4 to 32 times the baseline integration time step. Speed increases and accuracy decreases with increased coarsening, with $r^2 = 0.24$ in accuracy lost for every factor of 10 gained in speed, reaching a maximum 92$\times$ speedup while maintaining $r^2 = 0.60$. We deliberately trained our solvers only on January ground-level wind data to examine their ability to generalize across seasons and vertical heights. The 4$\times$-coarsened learned solver successfully reproduces simulations over 72 vertical levels. The 8$\times$--16$\times$ solvers (but not 32$\times$) emulate most vertical levels. The learned solvers also generalize well across seasons, except for instabilities in June and October. With additional fine-tuning, these learned solvers could be appropriate for operational use where trading accuracy for speed could be advantageous, such as in screening tools, in ensemble simulations, or with data assimilation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于时间粗粒化的机器学习求解器，在不牺牲空间分辨率的前提下加速大气平流模拟，最高实现92倍加速。
ai.motivation: 现有机器学习平流代理模型要么加速有限，要么牺牲空间分辨率，需要一种兼顾分辨率与速度的方法。
ai.method: 使用卷积神经网络，以浓度和CFL数为输入，输出质量通量；训练模型采用比CFL条件允许的更大的积分步长（时间粗粒化）。
ai.result: 在10天地面水平平流模拟中，时间粗化4-32倍时R²为0.60-0.98；速度每提高10倍精度损失R²约0.24，最大92倍加速时R²=0.60；模型能泛化到不同季节和垂直层。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在保持空间分辨率下显著加速平流计算的方法，适用于需要速度与精度权衡的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将时间粗粒化与物理信息神经网络结合，在训练中嵌入CFL约束，可能进一步提升泛化性和稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 粗化因子过大时精度下降明显，且在某些月份出现不稳定性，需谨慎选择粗化倍数或引入自适应机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受CFL条件启发，直接让模型学习更大时间步长下的物理演化，突破传统数值方法的步长限制。

en.tldr: A machine-learned solver using temporal coarse-graining accelerates horizontal advection simulations without sacrificing spatial resolution, achieving up to 92× speedup.
en.motivation: Existing ML surrogate models for advection either achieve limited speedup or sacrifice spatial resolution; a method preserving resolution while gaining speed is needed.
en.method: A convolutional neural network takes concentrations and CFL numbers as inputs and outputs mass flux; trained to take larger integration steps than the CFL condition allows (temporal coarse-graining).
en.result: On 10-day ground-level horizontal advection, temporal coarsening factors of 4–32 yield R² 0.60–0.98; speed increases by 10× cost R² 0.24, max 92× speedup at R² 0.60; generalizes across seasons and vertical levels.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to significantly accelerate advection computations while maintaining spatial resolution, useful in scenarios requiring speed-accuracy trade-offs.

idea_spark.idea: Combine temporal coarse-graining with physics-informed neural networks, embedding CFL constraints during training to improve generalization and stability.
idea_spark.risk: Large coarsening factors cause significant accuracy loss and instabilities in certain months; careful selection or adaptive mechanisms are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the CFL condition, directly train the model to learn physical evolution at larger time steps, breaking the step-size limit of traditional methods.

papers.title: Support-Proximity Augmented Diffusion Estimation for Offline Black-Box Optimization
papers.summary: Offline black-box optimization aims to discover novel designs with high property scores using only a static dataset, a task fundamentally challenged by the out-of-distribution (OOD) extrapolation problem. Existing approaches typically bifurcate into inverse methods, which struggle with the ill-posed nature of mapping scores to designs, and forward methods, which often lack the distributional expressivity to quantify uncertainty effectively. In this work, we propose SPADE (Support-Proximity Augmented Diffusion Estimation), a novel framework that reimagines forward surrogate modeling through the lens of conditional generative modeling. SPADE models the forward likelihood p(y|x) using a diffusion model, but with two critical enhancements to tailor it for optimization: (1) a Calibrated Diffusion Estimation module that enforces global consistency in statistical moments and pairwise rankings, and (2) a Support-Proximity Regularization mechanism that implicitly internalizes the data manifold constraint p(x) via kNN-based density estimation. Theoretically, we prove that our regularization is first-order equivalent to maximizing a Bayesian posterior with a valid design prior. Empirically, SPADE achieves state-of-the-art performance across Design-Bench tasks and an LLM data mixture optimization benchmark.

ai.tldr: 提出SPADE框架，通过扩散模型和两种增强机制解决离线黑箱优化中的分布外问题。
ai.motivation: 现有逆方法和前向方法分别面临病态映射和不确定性量化不足的挑战。
ai.method: 使用扩散模型建模前向似然，并引入校准扩散估计和支持邻近正则化。
ai.result: 在Design-Bench和LLM数据混合优化基准上达到最先进性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需在线交互即可优化黑箱函数的新方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用kNN密度估计隐式编码数据流形约束。
idea_spark.risk: kNN在高维空间可能失效，需注意维度灾难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将贝叶斯后验最大化与一阶等价性作为理论支撑。

en.tldr: Proposes SPADE, a diffusion-based framework with two enhancements to address out-of-distribution extrapolation in offline black-box optimization.
en.motivation: Existing inverse and forward methods suffer from ill-posed mapping and insufficient uncertainty quantification, respectively.
en.method: Models forward likelihood via a diffusion model, augmented with calibrated diffusion estimation and support-proximity regularization.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on Design-Bench and an LLM data mixture optimization benchmark.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new method for optimizing black-box functions without online interaction.

idea_spark.idea: Implicitly encodes data manifold constraints via kNN density estimation.
idea_spark.risk: kNN may fail in high-dimensional spaces due to the curse of dimensionality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Theoretical equivalence to Bayesian posterior maximization provides a solid foundation.

papers.title: Neural-Schwarz Tiling for Geometry-Universal PDE Solving at Scale
papers.summary: Most learned PDE solvers follow a global-surrogate paradigm: a neural operator is trained to map full problem descriptions to full solution fields for a prescribed distribution of geometries, boundary conditions, and coefficients. This has enabled fast inference within fixed problem families, but limits reuse across new domains and makes large-scale deployment dependent on expensive problem-specific data generation. We introduce $\textbf{NEST}$ ($\textbf{Ne}$ural-$\textbf{S}$chwarz $\textbf{T}$iling), a local-to-global framework that shifts learning from full-domain solution operators to reusable local physical solvers. The central premise is that, although global PDE solutions depend on geometry, scale, and boundary conditions, the physical response on small neighborhoods can be learned locally and composed into global solutions through classical domain decomposition. NEST learns a neural operator on minimal voxel patches ($3 \times 3 \times 3$) with diverse local geometries and boundary/interface data. At inference time, an unseen voxelized domain is tiled into overlapping patches, the learned local solver is applied patchwise, and global consistency is enforced through iterative Schwarz coupling with partition-of-unity assembly. In this way, generalization is shifted from a monolithic neural model to the combination of local physics learning and algorithmic global assembly. We instantiate NEST on nonlinear static equilibrium in compressible neo-Hookean solids and evaluate it on large, geometrically complex 3D domains far outside the scale of the training patches. Our results show that local neural building blocks, coupled through Schwarz iteration, offer a reusable local-training path toward scalable learned PDE solvers that generalize across domain size, shape, and boundary-condition configurations.

ai.tldr: 提出NEST框架，通过局部神经求解器与Schwarz迭代组合，实现跨几何、跨尺度的PDE求解。
ai.motivation: 全局神经算子方法依赖特定问题数据，难以泛化到新几何和大规模域。
ai.method: 在极小体素块上训练局部神经算子，推理时通过重叠块平铺和Schwarz迭代组装全局解。
ai.result: 在可压缩neo-Hookean固体非线性静平衡问题上，NEST能泛化到远大于训练块的复杂3D域。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可复用的局部训练策略，降低大规模PDE求解的数据生成成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将体素块视为“物理基元”，通过组合不同局部算子实现多物理场耦合。
idea_spark.risk: Schwarz迭代收敛性可能依赖问题类型，且局部算子对边界条件敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典区域分解算法启发，将深度学习与数值方法结合，实现可扩展的神经求解器。

zh.tldr: 提出NEST框架，通过局部神经求解器与Schwarz迭代组合，实现跨几何、跨尺度的PDE求解。
zh.motivation: 全局神经算子方法依赖特定问题数据，难以泛化到新几何和大规模域。

idea_spark.risk: Schwarz迭代收敛性可能依赖问题类型，且局部算子对边界条件敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典区域分解算法启发，将深度学习与数值方法结合，实现可扩展的神经求解器。

en.tldr: NEST learns local neural solvers on small voxel patches and assembles global PDE solutions via Schwarz iteration, enabling generalization across geometry and scale.
en.motivation: Global neural operators require expensive problem-specific data and fail to generalize to new geometries or large domains.
en.method: Train a neural operator on 3×3×3 voxel patches with diverse local geometries; at inference, tile the domain into overlapping patches, apply the local solver, and enforce consistency via Schwarz coupling.
en.result: On nonlinear static equilibrium of compressible neo-Hookean solids, NEST generalizes to large, geometrically complex 3D domains far beyond training patch scale.
en.help_to_user: Offers a reusable local-training path that reduces data generation cost for large-scale PDE solving.

idea_spark.idea: Treat voxel patches as 'physical primitives' and combine different local operators for multi-physics coupling.
idea_spark.risk: Schwarz iteration convergence may depend on problem type, and local operators are sensitive to boundary conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by classical domain decomposition, combining deep learning with numerical methods for scalable neural PDE solvers.

papers.title: Approximation of Maximally Monotone Operators : A Graph Convergence Perspective
papers.summary: Operator learning has been highly successful for continuous mappings between infinite-dimensional spaces, such as PDE solution operators. However, many operators of interest-including differential operators-are discontinuous or set-valued, and lie outside classical approximation frameworks. We propose a paradigm shift by formulating approximation via graph convergence (Painlevé-Kuratowski convergence), which is well-suited for closed operators. We show that uniform and $L^p$ approximation are fundamentally inadequate in this setting. Focusing on maximally monotone operators, we prove that any such operator can be approximated in the sense of local graph convergence by continuous encoder-decoder architectures, and further construct structure-preserving approximations that retain maximal monotonicity via resolvent-based parameterizations.

ai.tldr: 提出通过图收敛（Painlevé-Kuratowski收敛）来逼近极大单调算子，证明连续编码器-解码器架构可在局部图收敛意义下逼近任意极大单调算子，并构造保持极大单调性的结构保持逼近。
ai.motivation: 许多算子（如微分算子）不连续或集值，经典逼近框架（均匀逼近、L^p逼近）不适用，需要新的逼近范式。

ai.help_to_user: 为处理不连续或集值算子的学习问题提供了理论基础和可行架构。

en.method: Reformulates approximation as graph convergence, leverages resolvent-based parameterizations of maximally monotone operators to build structure-preserving approximations.
en.result: Proves that any maximally monotone operator can be approximated by continuous encoder-decoder architectures under local graph convergence, and provides explicit constructions that preserve maximal monotonicity.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical foundations and feasible architectures for learning discontinuous or set-valued operators.

idea_spark.risk: Local graph convergence may not guarantee global approximation accuracy; practical training needs to balance convergence and computational efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from variational analysis and convex optimization, importing convergence concepts from operator theory into deep learning.

papers.title: NORI: Fast probabilistic inference for ambiguous observation-entity mappings
papers.summary: NORI performs probabilistic inference to resolve ambiguous mappings between experimental observations and biological entities orders of magnitude faster than state-of-the-art methods. This makes large-scale analysis and extensive hyperparameter optimization possible, and supports a broader range of bioinformatics applications, including protein inference, taxonomic and functional analysis in omics-fields.

ai.tldr: NORI 是一种概率推理方法，用于解决实验观测与生物实体之间的模糊映射问题，速度比现有方法快数个数量级。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在处理大规模观测-实体映射时速度慢，限制了超参数优化和广泛应用。
ai.method: 采用概率推理框架，高效处理模糊映射。
ai.result: 速度比最先进方法快数个数量级，支持大规模分析和超参数优化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速解决蛋白质推断、分类和功能分析中的映射模糊问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将 NORI 应用于单细胞 RNA-seq 数据中的细胞类型映射。
idea_spark.risk: 可能需要对概率模型进行领域特定调整，且计算资源需求可能较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从超参数优化中受益，启发开发自适应推理算法。

en.method: Uses a probabilistic inference framework to efficiently handle ambiguous mappings.

papers.title: Beyond Similarity: Temporal Operator Attention for Time Series Analysis
papers.summary: A persistent paradox in time-series forecasting is that structurally simple MLP and linear models often outperform high-capacity Transformers. We argue that this gap arises from a mismatch in the sequence-modeling primitive: while many time-series dynamics are governed by global temporal operators (e.g., filtering and harmonic structure), standard attention forms each output as a convex combination of inputs. This restricts its ability to represent signed and oscillatory transformations that are fundamental to temporal signal processing. We formalize this limitation as a simplex-constrained mixing bottleneck in softmax attention, which becomes especially restrictive for operator-driven time-series tasks. To address this, we propose $\textbf{Temporal Operator Attention (TOA)}$, a framework that augments attention with explicit, learnable sequence-space operators, enabling direct signed mixing across time while preserving input-dependent adaptivity. To make dense $N \times N$ operators practical, we introduce Stochastic Operator Regularization, a high-variance dropout mechanism that stabilizes training and prevents trivial memorization. Across forecasting, anomaly detection, and classification benchmarks, TOA consistently improves performance when integrated into standard backbones such as PatchTST and iTransformer, with particularly strong gains in reconstruction-heavy tasks. These results suggest that explicit operator learning is a key ingredient for effective time-series modeling.

ai.tldr: 提出时间算子注意力（TOA），通过显式可学习的序列空间算子增强注意力机制，解决标准注意力在时间序列建模中的符号混合限制。
ai.motivation: 标准注意力将输出限制为输入的凸组合，无法表示时间序列中关键的符号和振荡变换，导致简单MLP/线性模型常优于Transformer。
ai.method: 在注意力中引入可学习的序列空间算子，实现直接的符号混合，并设计随机算子正则化（高方差dropout）稳定训练。
ai.result: 集成到PatchTST、iTransformer等骨干中，在预测、异常检测和分类任务上持续提升性能，尤其在重建密集型任务中增益显著。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种改进时间序列Transformer的新注意力机制，可直接替换现有模型中的标准注意力。

idea_spark.idea: 将TOA与图神经网络结合，处理时空序列数据中的复杂算子关系。
idea_spark.risk: 算子参数化可能增加模型复杂度，需谨慎设计正则化避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受信号处理中全局算子（滤波、谐波结构）启发，将传统信号处理工具融入深度学习注意力。

en.tldr: Proposes Temporal Operator Attention (TOA), augmenting attention with learnable sequence-space operators to overcome the convex-combination limitation of standard attention for time series.
en.motivation: Standard attention forms outputs as convex combinations of inputs, failing to represent signed and oscillatory transformations essential for temporal signal processing, causing simple models to outperform Transformers.
en.method: Introduces explicit learnable sequence-space operators into attention for direct signed mixing, along with Stochastic Operator Regularization (high-variance dropout) for stable training.
en.result: When integrated into backbones like PatchTST and iTransformer, TOA consistently improves performance on forecasting, anomaly detection, and classification, with strong gains in reconstruction-heavy tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a drop-in attention mechanism that enhances time-series Transformers by enabling operator-driven temporal mixing.

idea_spark.idea: Combine TOA with graph neural networks to model complex operator relationships in spatiotemporal sequences.
idea_spark.risk: Operator parameterization may increase model complexity; careful regularization is needed to avoid overfitting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by global operators (filtering, harmonic structure) in signal processing, integrating traditional tools into deep learning attention.

papers.title: Sharpen Your Flow: Sharpness-Aware Sampling for Flow Matching
papers.summary: Flow matching models generate samples by numerically integrating a learned velocity field, with each integration step requiring a neural network evaluation. Fast generation therefore requires using a small fixed evaluation budget effectively: the key question is not only how to integrate the flow, but where the sampler should spend its steps. We propose SharpEuler, a training-free sampler that profiles a pretrained model offline by estimating where the learned velocity field changes most rapidly along calibration trajectories. This finite-difference estimate defines a solver-aware sharpness profile, which is smoothed and converted by a quantile transform into a timestep grid for any desired inference budget. At test time, sampling remains ordinary Euler integration with the same number of model evaluations as a uniform schedule. We justify SharpEuler using three principles: a numerical principle identifying trajectory acceleration as the leading source of Euler discretization error, a variational principle deriving sharpness-based power-law timestep densities, and a statistical guarantee showing that the finite-sample calibrated sampler is stable at the terminal distribution level. Our experiments show that SharpEuler improves sample quality at fixed budgets, reducing inter-mode leakage and increasing mode coverage.

ai.tldr: 提出SharpEuler，一种无需训练的采样方法，通过离线分析速度场变化率来优化流匹配模型的积分步长分配，提升生成质量。
ai.motivation: 流匹配模型生成样本需要多次神经网络评估，固定预算下如何有效分配积分步长是关键问题。
ai.method: 离线校准：用有限差分估计速度场变化率，构建锐度分布，经平滑和分位数变换生成自适应时间步网格；推理时使用普通欧拉积分。
ai.result: 在固定评估预算下，SharpEuler减少了模式间泄漏，增加了模式覆盖，提升了样本质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在不增加计算成本的前提下，提升流匹配模型的生成效果。

idea_spark.idea: 将数值积分中的局部误差估计转化为采样调度，实现计算资源的高效利用。
idea_spark.risk: 离线校准依赖代表性轨迹，可能对分布外数据不鲁棒。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数值分析中的自适应步长控制出发，结合统计校准，为生成模型提供理论驱动的采样方案。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从数值分析中的自适应步长控制出发，结合统计校准，为生成模型提供理论驱动的采样方案。

en.tldr: Proposes SharpEuler, a training-free sampling method that optimizes step allocation for flow matching by offline profiling of velocity field changes, improving generation quality.
en.motivation: Flow matching models require multiple neural network evaluations per sample; the key is how to effectively allocate integration steps under a fixed budget.
en.method: Offline calibration: estimate velocity field change rate via finite differences, build a sharpness profile, smooth and quantile-transform it into an adaptive timestep grid; inference uses plain Euler integration.
en.result: Under fixed evaluation budgets, SharpEuler reduces inter-mode leakage and increases mode coverage, improving sample quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users improve generation quality of flow matching models without extra computational cost.

idea_spark.idea: Convert local error estimates from numerical integration into a sampling schedule for efficient computation.
idea_spark.risk: Offline calibration relies on representative trajectories and may be less robust to out-of-distribution data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from adaptive step control in numerical analysis and combines it with statistical calibration for a theory-driven sampling scheme in generative models.

papers.title: Fast and Accurate Prediction of Lattice Thermal Conductivity via Machine Learning Surrogates
papers.summary: The appearance of generative models has opened vast chemical spaces in the design of functional materials. Although machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) have substantially accelerated phonon calculations, high-fidelity prediction of lattice thermal conductivity \k{appa}lat still requires accurate treatment of anharmonic interactions, which remains a key challenge for existing potentials across novel chemical spaces. To address this challenge, we present a comprehensive benchmark of 15 surrogate models for predicting \k{appa}lat using the Phonix database, which contains 6,966 entries with anharmonic phonon properties derived from first-principles calculations. Firstly, We categorize these surrogate models into three distinct groups: Physical-informed feature descriptors combined with ML models, end-to-end deep neural networks, and pre-trained MLIP-embeddings combined with ML models. By evaluating model performance across random, space-group disjoint (testing generalization to unseen crystal symmetries), and Out-Of-Distribution splits (OOD dataset that testing extrapolation to property regimes beyond the training range) based on \k{appa}lat, we probe both interpolation and exploration capabilities. Our results reveal that MLIP-embedded models excel in interpolation within well-sampled regions, deep neural network models especially ALiEGNN demonstrate superior robustness in OOD regimes critical for discovering novel low-\k{appa}lat. Additionally, we find a systematic degradation in performance when the structural representation is reduced. Although surrogate models exhibit lower accuracy than direct simulations using first-principles calculation, they reduce computational costs by orders of magnitude, enabling efficient high-throughput screening of thermoelectric materials with minimal loss in generative design workflows.

ai.tldr: 提出15种机器学习代理模型，基于Phonix数据库预测晶格热导率，评估插值与外推能力，发现MLIP嵌入模型在插值中表现优异，ALiEGNN在外推中鲁棒性最强，计算成本降低数个数量级。
ai.motivation: 现有机器学习原子间势能（MLIP）在处理非简谐相互作用时精度不足，难以高保真预测新型化学空间中的晶格热导率。
ai.method: 将15种代理模型分为物理特征描述符+ML、端到端深度神经网络、预训练MLIP嵌入+ML三类，在随机、空间群分离、分布外三种数据划分下进行基准测试。
ai.result: MLIP嵌入模型在插值区域表现最佳，ALiEGNN在分布外区域鲁棒性最强；结构表示简化导致性能系统性下降；代理模型精度低于第一性原理但计算成本降低数个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速筛选低晶格热导率的热电材料，支持生成式设计流程中的高通量筛选。

idea_spark.idea: 结合主动学习策略，在分布外区域迭代采样，进一步提升代理模型对新化学空间的泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型在极端低热导率区域可能产生较大误差，需谨慎用于定量设计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受ALiEGNN在外推中鲁棒性的启发，可探索图神经网络与物理约束的融合，提升模型对非简谐效应的捕捉能力。

zh.result: MLIP嵌入模型在插值区域表现最佳，ALiEGNN在分布外区域鲁棒性最强；结构表示简化导致性能系统性下降；代理模型精度低于第一性原理但计算成本降低数个数量级。

idea_spark.idea: 结合主动学习策略，在分布外区域迭代采样，进一步提升代理模型对新化学空间的泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型在极端低热导率区域可能产生较大误差，需谨慎用于定量设计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受ALiEGNN在外推中鲁棒性的启发，可探索图神经网络与物理约束的融合，提升模型对非简谐效应的捕捉能力。

en.tldr: Benchmarked 15 surrogate models for predicting lattice thermal conductivity using the Phonix database, evaluating interpolation and extrapolation capabilities; MLIP-embedded models excel in interpolation, ALiEGNN shows superior robustness in out-of-distribution regimes, reducing computational cost by orders of magnitude.
en.motivation: Existing MLIPs struggle with accurate anharmonic interaction treatment for high-fidelity lattice thermal conductivity prediction in novel chemical spaces.
en.method: Categorized 15 surrogate models into three groups: physics-informed feature descriptors+ML, end-to-end deep neural networks, and pre-trained MLIP embeddings+ML; evaluated on random, space-group disjoint, and out-of-distribution splits.
en.result: MLIP-embedded models perform best in interpolation; ALiEGNN shows superior robustness in OOD regimes; performance degrades systematically with reduced structural representation; surrogate models are orders of magnitude faster than first-principles simulations.
en.help_to_user: Enables efficient high-throughput screening of thermoelectric materials with low lattice thermal conductivity in generative design workflows.

idea_spark.idea: Combine active learning to iteratively sample OOD regions, further improving generalization to novel chemical spaces.
idea_spark.risk: Surrogate models may have large errors in extremely low thermal conductivity regimes, caution needed for quantitative design.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by ALiEGNN's robustness in OOD, explore graph neural networks with physics-informed constraints to better capture anharmonic effects.

papers.title: Exact Stiefel Optimization for Probabilistic PLS: Closed-Form Updates, Error Bounds, and Calibrated Uncertainty
papers.summary: Probabilistic partial least squares (PPLS) is a central likelihood-based model for two-view learning when one needs both interpretable latent factors and calibrated uncertainty. Building on the identifiable parameterization of Bouhaddani et al.\ (2018), existing fitting pipelines still face two practical bottlenecks: noise--signal coupling under joint EM/ECM updates and nontrivial handling of orthogonality constraints. Following the fixed-noise scalar-likelihood line of Hu et al.\ (2025), we develop an end-to-end framework that combines noise pre-estimation, constrained likelihood optimization, and prediction calibration in one pipeline. Relative to Hu et al.\ (2025), we replace full-spectrum noise averaging with noise-subspace estimation and replace interior-point penalty handling with exact Stiefel-manifold optimization. The noise-subspace estimator attains a signal-strength-independent leading finite-sample rate and matches a minimax lower bound, while the full-spectrum estimator is shown to be inconsistent under the same model. We further extend the framework to sub-Gaussian settings via optional Gaussianization and provide closed-form standard errors through a block-structured Fisher analysis. Across synthetic high-noise settings and two multi-omics benchmarks (TCGA-BRCA and PBMC CITE-seq), the method achieves near-nominal coverage without post-hoc recalibration, reaches Ridge-level point accuracy on TCGA-BRCA at rank $r=3$, matches or exceeds PO2PLS on cross-view prediction while providing native calibrated uncertainty, and improves stability of parameter recovery.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于Stiefel流形精确优化的概率偏最小二乘框架，实现闭式更新、误差界和校准不确定性。
ai.motivation: 现有概率偏最小二乘方法存在噪声-信号耦合和正交约束处理困难，导致拟合效率低且不确定性校准不足。
ai.method: 采用噪声子空间预估计替代全谱噪声平均，利用Stiefel流形精确优化替代内点罚函数处理，并扩展至次高斯分布。
ai.result: 噪声子空间估计器达到信号强度无关的有限样本率并匹配极小化下界；在合成高噪声和多组学基准上实现接近名义覆盖率的校准不确定性，参数恢复稳定性提升。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可直接用于多视图学习（如多组学整合）的、具有校准不确定性的概率模型，无需后处理校准。

idea_spark.idea: 将噪声子空间估计与流形优化结合，可能适用于高噪声环境下的因果推断或缺失数据填补。
idea_spark.risk: 噪声子空间估计在极低信噪比下可能失效，需进一步理论分析。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受固定噪声标量似然线启发，但通过子空间估计和流形优化克服了原方法的局限性。

en.tldr: Proposes an exact Stiefel-manifold optimization framework for probabilistic partial least squares, achieving closed-form updates, error bounds, and calibrated uncertainty.
en.motivation: Existing probabilistic PLS methods suffer from noise-signal coupling and difficulty handling orthogonality constraints, leading to inefficient fitting and poor uncertainty calibration.
en.method: Replaces full-spectrum noise averaging with noise-subspace estimation, replaces interior-point penalty with exact Stiefel-manifold optimization, and extends to sub-Gaussian settings.
en.result: Noise-subspace estimator achieves signal-strength-independent finite-sample rate matching a minimax lower bound; achieves near-nominal coverage on synthetic and multi-omics benchmarks without post-hoc recalibration, with improved parameter recovery stability.
en.help_to_user: Provides a probabilistic model for multi-view learning (e.g., multi-omics integration) with calibrated uncertainty, eliminating the need for post-hoc recalibration.

idea_spark.idea: Combining noise-subspace estimation with manifold optimization may be applicable to causal inference or missing data imputation under high noise.
idea_spark.risk: Noise-subspace estimation may fail under extremely low signal-to-noise ratios, requiring further theoretical analysis.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the fixed-noise scalar-likelihood line, but overcomes its limitations via subspace estimation and manifold optimization.

papers.title: A Comparative Study of Model Selection Criteria for Symbolic Regression
papers.summary: Effective model selection is critical in symbolic regression (SR) to identify mathematical expressions that balance accuracy and complexity, and have low expected error on unseen data. Many modern implementations of genetic programming (GP) for SR generate a set of Pareto optimal candidate solutions, but reliable automatic selection of solutions that generalize well remains an open issue. Current literature offers various information-theoretic and Bayesian approaches, yet comprehensive comparisons of their performance across different data regimes are limited. This study presents a systematic empirical comparison of widely used selection criteria: the Akaike information criterion (AIC), the corrected AIC (AICc), the Bayesian information criterion (BIC), minimum description length (MDL), as well as Efron's bootstrap estimate for the in-sample prediction error on seven synthetic datasets with Gaussian noise. We rank candidate expressions generated by perturbing ground-truth functions to assess generalization error and selection probability of the ground-truth expression. Our findings reveal that MDL consistently identifies models with the lowest test error and the shortest length across most datasets. While no single criterion dominates all results, MDL and BIC produced the highest probability of selecting the ground-truth expressions.

ai.tldr: 系统比较了符号回归中多种模型选择准则（AIC、AICc、BIC、MDL及Efron自助法）在含噪合成数据集上的表现，发现MDL在测试误差和模型简洁性上表现最佳。
ai.motivation: 符号回归中自动选择泛化能力强的候选解仍是一个开放问题，现有文献缺乏对不同数据条件下多种信息准则的系统比较。
ai.method: 在七个含高斯噪声的合成数据集上，通过扰动真实函数生成候选表达式，使用AIC、AICc、BIC、MDL和Efron自助法进行排序，评估泛化误差和选择真实表达式的概率。
ai.result: MDL在大多数数据集上一致选出测试误差最低且长度最短的模型；MDL和BIC选择真实表达式的概率最高。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解不同模型选择准则在符号回归中的优劣，指导实际应用中准则的选择。

idea_spark.idea: 结合MDL与集成学习，构建更鲁棒的符号回归模型选择框架。
idea_spark.risk: MDL可能对复杂噪声结构不敏感，需在真实噪声分布未知时谨慎使用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受MDL在简洁性与泛化性间平衡的启发，可探索将MDL作为正则化项嵌入神经符号模型训练。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受MDL在简洁性与泛化性间平衡的启发，可探索将MDL作为正则化项嵌入神经符号模型训练。

en.tldr: A systematic comparison of model selection criteria (AIC, AICc, BIC, MDL, Efron's bootstrap) for symbolic regression on noisy synthetic datasets shows MDL consistently selects models with lowest test error and shortest length.
en.motivation: Reliable automatic selection of generalizable solutions in symbolic regression remains an open issue, and comprehensive comparisons across different data regimes are limited.
en.method: On seven synthetic datasets with Gaussian noise, candidate expressions are generated by perturbing ground-truth functions, then ranked using AIC, AICc, BIC, MDL, and Efron's bootstrap to assess generalization error and selection probability of the ground-truth expression.

idea_spark.idea: Combine MDL with ensemble learning to build a more robust symbolic regression model selection framework.

idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by MDL's balance between simplicity and generalization, explore using MDL as a regularization term in training neuro-symbolic models.

papers.title: Beyond Prediction: Interval Neural Networks for Uncertainty-Aware System Identification
papers.summary: System identification (SysID) is critical for modeling dynamical systems from experimental data, yet traditional approaches often fail to capture nonlinear behaviors. While deep learning offers powerful tools for modeling such dynamics, incorporating uncertainty quantification is essential to ensure reliable predictions. This paper presents a systematic framework for constructing and training interval Neural Networks (INNs) for uncertainty-aware SysID. By extending crisp neural networks into interval counterparts, we develop Interval LSTM and NODE models that propagate uncertainty through interval arithmetic without probabilistic assumptions. This design allows them to represent uncertainty and produce prediction intervals. For training, we propose two strategies: Cascade INN (C-INN), a two-stage approach converting a trained crisp NN into an INN, and Joint INN (J-INN), a one-stage framework jointly optimizing prediction accuracy and interval precision. Both strategies employ uncertainty-aware loss functions and parameterization tricks to ensure reliable learning. Comprehensive experiments on multiple SysID datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of both approaches and benchmark their performance against well-established uncertainty-aware baselines: C-INN achieves superior point prediction accuracy, whereas J-INN yields more accurate and better-calibrated prediction intervals. Furthermore, to reveal how uncertainty is represented across model parameters, the concept of channel-wise elasticity is introduced, which is used to identify distinct patterns across the two training strategies. The results of this study demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively integrates deep learning with uncertainty-aware modeling.

ai.tldr: 提出区间神经网络框架，用于不确定性感知的系统辨识，通过区间算术传播不确定性，无需概率假设。
ai.motivation: 传统系统辨识方法难以捕捉非线性行为，且缺乏不确定性量化，导致预测不可靠。
ai.method: 将标准神经网络扩展为区间神经网络，开发区间LSTM和区间NODE模型；提出级联INN和联合INN两种训练策略，采用不确定性感知损失函数和参数化技巧。
ai.result: 级联INN在点预测精度上更优，联合INN产生更准确且校准更好的预测区间；引入通道弹性概念揭示参数不确定性模式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户构建具有不确定性感知能力的系统辨识模型，提升预测可靠性。

idea_spark.risk: 区间算术可能增加计算开销，且训练稳定性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受区间算术和深度学习结合的启发，探索其他非概率不确定性表示方法。

en.tldr: Proposes an interval neural network framework for uncertainty-aware system identification, propagating uncertainty via interval arithmetic without probabilistic assumptions.
en.motivation: Traditional system identification methods struggle with nonlinear behaviors and lack uncertainty quantification, leading to unreliable predictions.

papers.title: Expected Batch Optimal Transport Plans and Consequences for Flow Matching
papers.summary: Solving optimal transport (OT) on random minibatches is a common surrogate for exact OT in large-scale learning. In flow matching (FM), this surrogate is used to obtain OT-like couplings that can straighten probability paths and reduce numerical integration cost. Yet, the population-level coupling induced by repeated minibatch OT remains only partially understood. We formalize this coupling as the expected batch OT plan $\overlineπ_{k}$, obtained by averaging empirical OT plans over independent minibatches of size $k$. We then establish its large-batch consistency and, in the semidiscrete case relevant to generative modeling, derive rates for both the transport-cost bias and the convergence of $\overlineπ_{k}$ to the OT plan. For FM, this yields a population coupling whose induced velocity field is regular enough to define a unique flow from the source to the discrete target. We finally quantify how OT batch size interacts with numerical integration in a tractable two-atom model and in synthetic and image experiments.

ai.tldr: 本文形式化了流匹配中重复小批量最优传输所诱导的期望批量OT计划，建立了其大样本一致性，并分析了传输成本偏差和收敛速率。
ai.motivation: 流匹配中使用小批量OT作为精确OT的替代，但其群体层面的耦合性质尚未完全理解。
ai.method: 将期望批量OT计划定义为独立小批量经验OT计划的平均，并在半离散情形下推导偏差和收敛速率。
ai.result: 证明了期望批量OT计划的大样本一致性，并给出了传输成本偏差的速率；在流匹配中，该计划诱导的速度场足够正则，可定义从源到离散目标的唯一流。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解小批量OT在流匹配中的统计行为，指导批量大小的选择以平衡计算与精度。

idea_spark.idea: 利用期望批量OT计划的正则性，设计更高效的数值积分策略。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果依赖于半离散假设，在完全连续情形下可能不直接适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将小批量OT的随机性视为一种隐式正则化，可能改善生成模型的泛化能力。

en.tldr: This paper formalizes the expected batch optimal transport plan induced by repeated minibatch OT in flow matching, establishes its large-batch consistency, and analyzes transport-cost bias and convergence rates.
en.motivation: Minibatch OT is used as a surrogate for exact OT in flow matching, but its population-level coupling is not fully understood.
en.method: Define the expected batch OT plan as the average of empirical OT plans over independent minibatches of size k, and derive bias and convergence rates in the semidiscrete case.
en.result: Prove large-batch consistency of the expected batch OT plan and provide rates for transport-cost bias; in flow matching, the induced velocity field is regular enough to define a unique flow from source to discrete target.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the statistical behavior of minibatch OT in flow matching, guiding batch size selection to balance computation and accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the regularity of the expected batch OT plan to design more efficient numerical integration strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical results rely on semidiscrete assumptions and may not directly apply in fully continuous settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Viewing the randomness of minibatch OT as implicit regularization may improve generalization of generative models.

papers.title: A nonlinear extension of parametric model embedding for dimensionality reduction in parametric shape design
papers.summary: Dimensionality reduction is essential in simulation-based shape design, where high-dimensional parameterizations hinder optimization, surrogate modeling, and systematic design-space exploration. Parametric Model Embedding (PME) addresses this issue by constructing reduced variables from geometric information while preserving an explicit backmapping to the original design parameters. However, PME is intrinsically linear and may become inefficient when the sampled design space is governed by nonlinear geometric variability. This paper introduces a nonlinear extension of PME, denoted NLPME. The proposed framework preserves the defining principle of PME -- geometry-driven latent variables and parameter-mediated reconstruction -- while replacing the linear reduced subspace with a nonlinear latent representation. Geometry is not reconstructed directly from the latent variables; instead, the latent representation is decoded into admissible design parameters, and the corresponding geometry is recovered through a forward parametric map. The method is assessed on a bio-inspired autonomous underwater glider with a 32-dimensional parametric shape description and a CAD-based geometry-generation process. NLPME reaches a 5\% reconstruction-error threshold with \(N=5\) latent variables, compared with \(N=8\) for linear PME, and a 1\% threshold with \(N=9\), compared with \(N=15\) for PME. Comparison with a deep autoencoder shows that most of the nonlinear compression gain can be retained while preserving an explicit backmapping to the original design variables. The results establish NLPME as a compact, admissible, and engineering-compatible nonlinear reduced representation for parametric shape design spaces.

ai.tldr: 提出非线性参数模型嵌入（NLPME），在保持显式反向映射的同时，用非线性潜变量替代线性子空间，在自主水下滑翔机形状设计中以更少潜变量达到更低重构误差。
ai.motivation: 线性参数模型嵌入（PME）在处理非线性几何变化时效率不足，需要更紧凑的非线性降维方法。
ai.method: 保留几何驱动潜变量和参数介导重构的核心思想，用非线性潜表示替换线性子空间，通过前向参数映射恢复几何。
ai.result: NLPME在5%重构误差阈值下仅需5个潜变量（PME需8个），1%阈值下需9个（PME需15个），且优于深度自编码器。
ai.help_to_user: 为参数化形状设计提供一种工程兼容的非线性降维方案，支持高效优化和设计空间探索。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理约束或几何先验进一步约束潜空间，提升重构的物理一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 非线性映射可能引入过拟合或局部极小，需更多数据或正则化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受PME线性局限启发，将几何信息与非线性降维结合，平衡压缩率与可解释性。

idea_spark.risk: 非线性映射可能引入过拟合或局部极小，需更多数据或正则化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受PME线性局限启发，将几何信息与非线性降维结合，平衡压缩率与可解释性。

en.tldr: Proposes Nonlinear Parametric Model Embedding (NLPME) that replaces linear subspace with nonlinear latent representation while preserving explicit backmapping, achieving lower reconstruction error with fewer latent variables in autonomous underwater glider shape design.
en.motivation: Linear PME becomes inefficient when design space exhibits nonlinear geometric variability, necessitating a more compact nonlinear reduction method.
en.method: Preserves geometry-driven latent variables and parameter-mediated reconstruction, replaces linear subspace with nonlinear latent representation, and recovers geometry via forward parametric mapping.
en.result: NLPME reaches 5% reconstruction error with N=5 (PME: N=8) and 1% with N=9 (PME: N=15), outperforming deep autoencoder while retaining explicit backmapping.
en.help_to_user: Provides an engineering-compatible nonlinear reduced representation for parametric shape design, enabling efficient optimization and design space exploration.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate physical constraints or geometric priors to further regularize the latent space for improved physical consistency.
idea_spark.risk: Nonlinear mapping may introduce overfitting or local minima, requiring more data or regularization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the linear limitation of PME, combining geometric information with nonlinear dimensionality reduction balances compression rate and interpretability.

papers.title: Is Monotonic Sampling Necessary in Diffusion Models?
papers.summary: Diffusion models generate samples by iteratively denoising a Gaussian prior, traversing a sequence of noise levels that, in every published sampler, decreases monotonically. Six years of intensive work has refined nearly every aspect of this recipe, including the corruption operator, the training objective, the schedule shape, the architecture, and the ODE solver. Yet the assumption of monotonicity itself has never been systematically tested. Here we ask whether monotonic sampling is load-bearing or merely conventional. We design four families of structured nonmonotonic schedules and apply them to three architecturally distinct generative models, DDPM, EDM, and Flow Matching, across NFE budgets ranging from 10 to 200 function evaluations, plus a 42-cell hyperparameter ablation, on CIFAR-10. Across all 90 tested configurations, no tested nonmonotonic schedule improves on the monotonic baseline. The magnitude of the penalty, however, spans nearly three orders of magnitude: persistent and substantial in DDPM, intermediate in Flow Matching, and indistinguishable from zero in EDM. We show that this variation is not noise but a structural property of each trained denoiser, and we formalize it as the Schedule Sensitivity Coefficient, a cheap, architecture-agnostic diagnostic that provides evidence of non-convergence to the Bayes-optimal denoiser at the critical noise level. Our findings justify the field's tacit reliance on monotonic schedules and supply a new probe of diffusion model quality complementary to sample-quality metrics such as Frechet Inception Distance.

ai.tldr: 本文系统检验了扩散模型中单调采样假设的必要性，发现非单调调度无法超越单调基线，并提出了调度敏感系数作为模型质量诊断工具。
ai.motivation: 扩散模型的所有采样器均采用单调递减噪声调度，但这一假设从未被系统验证，作者旨在探究单调性是否必要。
ai.method: 设计了四类结构化非单调调度，在DDPM、EDM和Flow Matching三种模型上，对CIFAR-10数据集进行90种配置的消融实验，并引入调度敏感系数。
ai.result: 所有非单调调度均未优于单调基线，但性能损失幅度差异巨大：DDPM显著，Flow Matching中等，EDM几乎为零。调度敏感系数可预测此差异。
ai.help_to_user: 确认单调采样是扩散模型的有效默认选择，并提供调度敏感系数作为评估模型收敛性的廉价诊断工具。

idea_spark.idea: 利用调度敏感系数指导扩散模型训练，在关键噪声水平强制优化去噪器，可能提升模型性能。
idea_spark.risk: 非单调调度在特定模型或任务上可能仍有潜力，当前结论局限于CIFAR-10和三种架构。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将单调性假设视为可测试的工程约束，启发其他领域（如强化学习中的探索调度）重新审视默认假设。

en.tldr: This paper systematically tests the necessity of monotonic sampling in diffusion models, finding that nonmonotonic schedules never outperform the monotonic baseline, and proposes the Schedule Sensitivity Coefficient as a diagnostic tool.
en.motivation: All published diffusion samplers use monotonically decreasing noise schedules, but this assumption has never been systematically tested; the authors aim to determine if monotonicity is essential.
en.method: Four families of structured nonmonotonic schedules are designed and tested on DDPM, EDM, and Flow Matching models using CIFAR-10 across 90 configurations, with a 42-cell hyperparameter ablation, and the Schedule Sensitivity Coefficient is introduced.
en.result: No nonmonotonic schedule improves over the monotonic baseline, but the penalty magnitude varies widely: large for DDPM, moderate for Flow Matching, and negligible for EDM. The Schedule Sensitivity Coefficient predicts this variation.
en.help_to_user: Confirms monotonic sampling as a safe default for diffusion models and provides a cheap diagnostic (Schedule Sensitivity Coefficient) for assessing model convergence.

idea_spark.idea: Use the Schedule Sensitivity Coefficient to guide training by enforcing optimization at critical noise levels, potentially improving model performance.
idea_spark.risk: Nonmonotonic schedules might still benefit specific models or tasks; current results are limited to CIFAR-10 and three architectures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treating monotonicity as a testable engineering constraint inspires re-evaluation of default assumptions in other fields (e.g., exploration schedules in reinforcement learning).

papers.title: Transferable Delay-Aware Reinforcement Learning via Implicit Causal Graph Modeling
papers.summary: Random delays weaken the temporal correspondence between actions and subsequent state feedback, making it difficult for agents to identify the true propagation process of action effects. In cross-task scenarios, changes in task objectives and reward formulations further reduce the reusability of previously acquired task knowledge. To address this problem, this paper proposes a transferable delay-aware reinforcement learning method based on implicit causal graph modeling. The proposed method uses a field-node encoder to represent high-dimensional observations as latent states with node-level semantics, and employs a message-passing mechanism to characterize dynamic causal dependencies among nodes, thereby learning transferable structured representations and environment dynamics knowledge. On this basis, imagination-driven behavior learning and planning are incorporated to optimize policies in the latent space, enabling cross-task knowledge transfer and rapid adaptation. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms baseline methods on DMC continuous control tasks with random delays. Cross-task transfer experiments further demonstrate that the learned structured representations and dynamics knowledge can be effectively transferred to new tasks and significantly accelerate policy adaptation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于隐式因果图建模的可迁移延迟感知强化学习方法，通过结构化表示和动力学知识实现跨任务快速适应。
ai.motivation: 随机延迟破坏动作与状态反馈的时序对应，跨任务场景中任务目标和奖励变化降低知识复用性。
ai.method: 使用场节点编码器将高维观测表示为具有节点级语义的潜在状态，通过消息传递机制建模节点间动态因果依赖，学习可迁移的结构化表示和环境动力学；结合想象驱动的行为学习与规划优化策略。
ai.result: 在DMC连续控制任务中优于基线方法，跨任务迁移实验表明结构化表示和动力学知识可有效迁移并加速策略适应。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种处理随机延迟和跨任务迁移的强化学习框架，适用于机器人控制等连续决策场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将因果图建模与想象驱动规划结合，在潜在空间实现高效策略优化。
idea_spark.risk: 隐式因果图可能无法完全捕捉复杂环境中的真实因果关系，导致迁移性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴因果推断和图神经网络，为延迟感知强化学习提供新视角。

en.tldr: Proposes a transferable delay-aware reinforcement learning method via implicit causal graph modeling, enabling cross-task rapid adaptation through structured representations and dynamics knowledge.
en.motivation: Random delays disrupt temporal correspondence between actions and state feedback; cross-task changes in objectives and rewards reduce knowledge reusability.
en.method: Uses a field-node encoder to represent high-dimensional observations as latent states with node-level semantics, employs message-passing to model dynamic causal dependencies, and incorporates imagination-driven behavior learning and planning for policy optimization in latent space.
en.result: Outperforms baselines on DMC continuous control tasks with random delays; cross-task transfer experiments show effective transfer of structured representations and dynamics knowledge, significantly accelerating policy adaptation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework for handling random delays and cross-task transfer in reinforcement learning, applicable to continuous decision-making scenarios like robot control.

idea_spark.idea: Combines causal graph modeling with imagination-driven planning for efficient policy optimization in latent space.
idea_spark.risk: Implicit causal graphs may not fully capture true causal relationships in complex environments, potentially degrading transfer performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws on causal inference and graph neural networks to offer a new perspective for delay-aware reinforcement learning.

papers.title: Discovery of Interpretable Surrogates via Agentic AI: Application to Gravitational Waves
papers.summary: Fast surrogate models for expensive simulations are now essential across the sciences, yet they typically operate as black boxes. We present \texttt{GWAgent}, a large language model (LLM)-based workflow that constructs interpretable analytic surrogates directly from simulation data. Surrogate modeling is well suited to agentic workflows because candidate models can be quantitatively validated against ground-truth simulations at each iteration. As a demonstration, we build a surrogate for gravitational waveforms from eccentric binary black hole mergers. We show that providing the agent with a physics-informed domain ansatz substantially improves output model accuracy. The resulting analytic surrogate attains a median Advanced LIGO mismatch of $6.9\times10^{-4}$ together with an $\sim 8.4\times$ speedup in waveform evaluation, surpassing both symbolic regression and conventional machine learning baselines. Beyond producing an accurate model, the workflow identifies compact physical structure from the learned representation. As an astrophysical application, we use \texttt{GWAgent} to analyze the eccentricity of GW200129 and infer $e_{20\mathrm{Hz}}=0.099^{+0.063}_{-0.044}$. These results show that validation-constrained agentic workflows can produce accurate, fast, and interpretable surrogates for scientific simulations and inference.

ai.tldr: 提出基于大语言模型的智能体工作流GWAgent，从模拟数据中构建可解释的解析替代模型，应用于引力波波形建模。
ai.motivation: 传统替代模型是黑箱，缺乏可解释性；而解析模型可提供物理洞察。
ai.method: 利用LLM智能体迭代生成候选解析模型，并通过与真实模拟的定量验证来优化；引入物理启发的领域假设提升精度。
ai.result: 对偏心双黑洞并合引力波波形，达到中位Advanced LIGO失配6.9×10⁻⁴，波形评估加速约8.4倍，优于符号回归和传统机器学习基线；并成功分析GW200129的偏心率。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种利用LLM智能体自动发现可解释解析模型的方法，适用于科学模拟和推理。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理先验与LLM智能体迭代验证，自动发现简洁解析公式。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM生成候选模型的质量，可能陷入局部最优；物理先验的选取需要领域知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将验证约束融入智能体循环，使模型生成过程透明且可解释。

en.tldr: We present GWAgent, an LLM-based agentic workflow that constructs interpretable analytic surrogates from simulation data, demonstrated on gravitational waveforms from eccentric binary black hole mergers.
en.motivation: Fast surrogate models are often black boxes; interpretable analytic surrogates can provide physical insights.
en.method: An LLM agent iteratively proposes candidate analytic models, validated against ground-truth simulations; a physics-informed domain ansatz improves accuracy.
en.result: The surrogate achieves median Advanced LIGO mismatch of 6.9×10⁻⁴ and ~8.4× speedup, outperforming symbolic regression and ML baselines; applied to infer eccentricity of GW200129.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to automatically discover interpretable analytic surrogates using LLM agents, useful for scientific simulation and inference.

idea_spark.idea: Combine physics priors with LLM agent iterative validation to discover compact analytic formulas.
idea_spark.risk: Quality depends on LLM candidate generation; may get stuck in local optima; physics priors require domain expertise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embed validation constraints into the agent loop to make model generation transparent and interpretable.

papers.title: Nonlinear filtering with stochastic discontinuities
papers.summary: Filtering problems with jumps in both the signal and the observation have been extensively studied, typically under the assumption that jump times are totally inaccessible. In many applications, however, jump times are known in advance (i.e., predictable), such as scheduled clinical visits, dividend payment dates, or inspection times in engineering systems. Taking predictable jump times as a starting point, we investigate a filtering problem in which both the signal and the observations can exhibit jumps at predictable times. We derive the corresponding Kushner-Stratonovich and Zakai equations, thereby extending classical nonlinear filtering results to a setting with predictable discontinuities. We illustrate the framework on a Kalman filtering model with predictable jumps and on applications to longitudinal clinical studies, such as spinal muscular atrophy (SMA), as well as to machine learning models (neural jump ODEs) and credit risk.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了信号和观测在可预测跳跃时间同时发生跳跃的非线性滤波问题，推导了相应的Kushner-Stratonovich和Zakai方程，并应用于卡尔曼滤波、纵向临床研究（如脊髓性肌萎缩症）、神经跳跃ODE和信用风险。
ai.motivation: 现有滤波理论主要处理完全不可及跳跃时间，但许多实际场景（如临床随访、股息支付、工程检查）中跳跃时间可提前预知，需要扩展经典非线性滤波结果。
ai.method: 以可预测跳跃时间为起点，推导信号和观测在可预测时刻同时跳跃时的Kushner-Stratonovich和Zakai方程。
ai.result: 建立了可预测跳跃下的非线性滤波框架，并通过卡尔曼滤波模型、纵向临床研究、神经跳跃ODE和信用风险案例验证了有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理可预测跳跃的滤波问题提供了理论工具，适用于临床数据分析、金融风险建模和机器学习中的跳跃过程。

idea_spark.idea: 将可预测跳跃滤波与深度概率模型结合，开发能同时处理已知和未知跳跃时间的混合滤波算法。
idea_spark.risk: 可预测跳跃假设可能不适用于完全随机跳跃场景；实际应用中跳跃时间预测误差可能影响滤波性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受临床随访和股息支付等确定性事件启发，将可预测跳跃纳入滤波理论，拓展了经典结果的应用边界。

en.tldr: This paper investigates nonlinear filtering when both signal and observations jump at predictable times, derives the corresponding Kushner-Stratonovich and Zakai equations, and illustrates applications to Kalman filtering, longitudinal clinical studies (e.g., spinal muscular atrophy), neural jump ODEs, and credit risk.
en.motivation: Existing filtering theory mainly deals with totally inaccessible jump times, but many real-world scenarios (e.g., clinical visits, dividend payments, engineering inspections) have predictable jump times, requiring an extension of classical nonlinear filtering results.
en.method: Starting from predictable jump times, we derive the Kushner-Stratonovich and Zakai equations for filtering when both signal and observations exhibit jumps at predictable times.
en.result: A nonlinear filtering framework for predictable jumps is established, validated through Kalman filtering, longitudinal clinical studies, neural jump ODEs, and credit risk examples.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical tools for filtering problems with predictable jumps, applicable to clinical data analysis, financial risk modeling, and jump processes in machine learning.

idea_spark.idea: Combine predictable jump filtering with deep probabilistic models to develop hybrid filtering algorithms that handle both known and unknown jump times.
idea_spark.risk: The predictable jump assumption may not hold for completely random jumps; prediction errors in jump times could degrade filtering performance in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by deterministic events like clinical follow-ups and dividend payments, incorporating predictable jumps into filtering theory extends the applicability of classical results.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Protein Complex Prediction and Design
papers.summary: Accurately modeling and designing protein complex structures is a central problem in computational structural biology, with broad implications for understanding cellular function and developing therapeutics. This thesis investigates two fundamental aspects of this problem using deep learning: domain-specific architectures that capture the hierarchical nature of protein structures, and search algorithms that efficiently navigate the vast sequence spaces of protein complexes to identify interacting homologs for improving complex structure prediction and to design protein sequences.

ai.tldr: 本论文利用深度学习研究蛋白质复合物的结构预测与设计，重点包括层次化结构建模和序列空间搜索算法。
ai.motivation: 准确建模和设计蛋白质复合物结构是计算结构生物学的核心问题，对理解细胞功能和开发治疗药物至关重要。
ai.method: 采用领域特定的深度学习架构捕捉蛋白质结构的层次性，并开发搜索算法高效遍历蛋白质复合物的序列空间以识别相互作用同源物。
ai.result: 改进了蛋白质复合物结构预测的准确性，并实现了蛋白质序列的设计。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用深度学习解决蛋白质复合物的结构预测与设计问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将搜索算法与生成模型结合，直接优化蛋白质复合物的结合亲和力和稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 序列空间巨大可能导致搜索效率低，且预测的复合物结构可能缺乏实验验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受蛋白质结构层次性启发，可探索图神经网络在复合物界面预测中的应用。

zh.method: 采用领域特定的深度学习架构捕捉蛋白质结构的层次性，并开发搜索算法高效遍历蛋白质复合物的序列空间以识别相互作用同源物。
zh.result: 改进了蛋白质复合物结构预测的准确性，并实现了蛋白质序列的设计。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用深度学习解决蛋白质复合物的结构预测与设计问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将搜索算法与生成模型结合，直接优化蛋白质复合物的结合亲和力和稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 序列空间巨大可能导致搜索效率低，且预测的复合物结构可能缺乏实验验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受蛋白质结构层次性启发，可探索图神经网络在复合物界面预测中的应用。

en.tldr: This thesis investigates deep learning for protein complex prediction and design, focusing on hierarchical structure modeling and sequence space search algorithms.
en.motivation: Accurately modeling and designing protein complex structures is central to computational structural biology, with implications for understanding cellular function and developing therapeutics.
en.method: Domain-specific deep learning architectures capture the hierarchical nature of protein structures, and search algorithms efficiently navigate sequence spaces to identify interacting homologs for improved prediction and design.
en.result: Improved accuracy in protein complex structure prediction and enabled protein sequence design.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how deep learning can address protein complex prediction and design challenges.

idea_spark.idea: Combine search algorithms with generative models to directly optimize binding affinity and stability of protein complexes.
idea_spark.risk: Large sequence spaces may lead to low search efficiency, and predicted complex structures may lack experimental validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the hierarchical nature of protein structures, explore graph neural networks for interface prediction in complexes.

papers.title: Multi-Agent System Identification with Nonlinear Sheaf Diffusion
papers.summary: Local interaction laws governing multi-agent systems can be difficult to recover from trajectory data, even when the dynamics are observed faithfully. In systems governed by a nonlinear sheaf Laplacian -- a generalization of the graph Laplacian accommodating heterogeneous state spaces and asymmetric communication channels -- the coordination law is encoded by edge potential functions whose gradients produce the inter-agent forces. Because trajectory observations record node-state evolution, they expose only the aggregate effect of the edge forces at each node: distinct interaction laws that agree at the node level are indistinguishable from trajectory data alone. We show that the fundamental obstruction to recovery is topological, measured by sheaf cohomology, and that unique recovery from an unconstrained function class is possible if and only if this cohomology vanishes. When the obstruction is nontrivial, we show that recovery within a finite-dimensional parameterized class is possible precisely when a data-dependent information matrix is positive definite. Experiments validate the theory and illustrate that accurate trajectory reproduction need not certify recovery of the underlying interaction law.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了从轨迹数据中恢复多智能体系统局部交互规律的问题，揭示了由非线性层拉普拉斯算子编码的协调律恢复的拓扑障碍，并给出了唯一恢复的条件。
ai.motivation: 多智能体系统的局部交互规律难以从轨迹数据中直接恢复，即使动力学被忠实观测，因为节点状态演化只暴露了边缘力的聚合效应。
ai.method: 利用层上同调理论刻画恢复的拓扑障碍，并证明当障碍非平凡时，在有限维参数化类中恢复的充要条件是数据依赖的信息矩阵正定。
ai.result: 理论表明，当层上同调为零时，可从无约束函数类唯一恢复；否则需参数化类且信息矩阵正定。实验验证了理论，并指出精确的轨迹复现不一定保证交互规律的正确恢复。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解多智能体系统识别中的根本性拓扑限制，以及如何通过参数化方法在存在障碍时仍可能恢复交互规律。

idea_spark.idea: 利用层拉普拉斯算子的谱性质设计数据采集策略，以降低恢复交互规律所需的信息矩阵条件数。
idea_spark.risk: 参数化类选择不当可能导致模型偏差，且信息矩阵正定性条件在实际高维系统中可能难以满足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将拓扑数据分析与系统辨识结合，为复杂网络中的因果推断提供新视角。

en.tldr: This paper investigates the recovery of local interaction laws in multi-agent systems from trajectory data, revealing topological obstructions measured by sheaf cohomology and providing conditions for unique recovery.
en.motivation: Local interaction laws in multi-agent systems are difficult to recover from trajectory data because node-state evolution only reveals the aggregate effect of edge forces.
en.method: Sheaf cohomology is used to characterize the fundamental obstruction to recovery, and it is shown that recovery within a finite-dimensional parameterized class is possible iff a data-dependent information matrix is positive definite.
en.result: The theory shows that unique recovery from an unconstrained function class is possible iff the sheaf cohomology vanishes; otherwise, parameterized recovery requires a positive definite information matrix. Experiments validate the theory and show that accurate trajectory reproduction does not guarantee correct recovery of the interaction law.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the fundamental topological limitations in multi-agent system identification and how parameterized methods can still enable recovery despite obstructions.

idea_spark.idea: Design data acquisition strategies using spectral properties of the sheaf Laplacian to reduce the condition number of the information matrix needed for recovery.
idea_spark.risk: Improper choice of parameterized class may introduce model bias, and the positive definiteness condition may be hard to satisfy in high-dimensional real-world systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining topological data analysis with system identification offers a new perspective for causal inference in complex networks.

papers.title: Positive probability of explosion for stochastic heat equation with superlinear accretive reaction term and polynomially growing multiplicative noise
papers.summary: This paper studies the finite time explosion of the stochastic heat equation $\frac{\partial u}{\partial t}(t,x)=\frac{\partial^2}{\partial x^2} u(t,x)+(u(t,x))^β+σ(u(t,x))\dot{W}(t,x)$. We consider an interval $D=[-π,π]$ under periodic boundary condition where $\dot{W}(t,x)$ is a space-time white noise and $σ(u)\approx u^γ$ near $\infty$. Our results refine existing results by identifying behavior in a previously less understood regime, where we show that if $β\in(1,3),γ\in(\fracβ{2},\frac{β+3}{4})$ or $β>1,γ\in(0,\fracβ{2}]$ then mild solutions can explode with positive probability. This paper provides a partial characterization of the explosion behavior in an intermediate parameter regime, and contribute to the understanding of the interplay between the drift and diffusion terms.

ai.tldr: 研究随机热方程在超线性反应项和多项式增长乘性噪声下的有限时间爆炸，给出了正爆炸概率的参数条件。
ai.motivation: 填补先前研究中参数区域爆炸行为的空白，理解漂移与扩散项的相互作用。
ai.method: 分析随机热方程的温和解，利用概率方法证明在特定参数范围内爆炸以正概率发生。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解随机偏微分方程中爆炸行为的参数依赖关系。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑将参数范围扩展到更一般的非线性或高维空间。
idea_spark.risk: 高维情形下白噪声正则性不足，可能需改用色噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合大偏差理论精确刻画爆炸概率的渐近行为。

zh.tldr: 研究随机热方程在超线性反应项和多项式增长乘性噪声下的有限时间爆炸，给出了正爆炸概率的参数条件。
zh.motivation: 填补先前研究中参数区域爆炸行为的空白，理解漂移与扩散项的相互作用。
zh.method: 分析随机热方程的温和解，利用概率方法证明在特定参数范围内爆炸以正概率发生。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解随机偏微分方程中爆炸行为的参数依赖关系。

en.tldr: Studies finite-time explosion of stochastic heat equation with superlinear reaction and polynomially growing multiplicative noise, giving parameter conditions for positive explosion probability.
en.motivation: Fill gaps in explosion behavior in previously less understood parameter regimes and understand interplay between drift and diffusion.
en.method: Analyze mild solutions of stochastic heat equation using probabilistic methods to prove explosion with positive probability in certain parameter ranges.
en.result: For β∈(1,3),γ∈(β/2,(β+3)/4) or β>1,γ∈(0,β/2], mild solutions explode with positive probability.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand parameter dependence of explosion in stochastic PDEs.

idea_spark.idea: Consider extending parameter ranges to more general nonlinearities or higher dimensions.
idea_spark.risk: In higher dimensions, white noise may lack regularity; colored noise might be needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine large deviation theory to precisely characterize asymptotic explosion probability.

papers.title: Enabling Small-Signal Stability Analysis of Black-Box Voltage Source Converters in Large-Scale Modern Power Systems
papers.summary: Modern power systems increasingly rely on power electronic converters, yet many of these devices are provided as black-box models, limiting the applicability of conventional small-signal analysis (SSA) tools. This work presents a unified multi-variable fitted state-space (SSA-FITSS) methodology that enables accurate small-signal modeling of black-box Voltage Source Converters (VSCs) using frequency-domain (FD) identification, adaptive pole-expansion, and reduced-order realization. The method includes an automated state-interpretation strategy that assigns fitted states to representative control-loop categories based on their dominant frequency ranges, providing an approximate but meaningful physical interpretation of the identified dynamics. This capability allows extensive modal analysis, including eigenvalue sensitivities and participation factors, in systems where internal converter details are unavailable. The methodology is validated on a grid-following (GFL) VSC and applied to the New England system, which contains multiple black-box converters operating in both GFL and grid-forming (GFM) modes. Results show that the SSA-FITSS models accurately reproduce converter and system dynamics, support full eigenvalue-based analysis, and reveal stability limits under varying synchronous generation and GFL penetration levels. The approach overcomes key limitations of existing identification-based techniques by enabling scalable, interpretable, and system-wide stability assessment.

ai.tldr: 提出一种统一的多变量拟合状态空间方法，用于黑箱电压源换流器的小信号建模与稳定性分析。
ai.motivation: 现代电力系统大量使用电力电子换流器，但许多设备以黑箱模型提供，传统小信号分析工具难以适用。
ai.method: 采用频域辨识、自适应极点扩展和降阶实现，构建拟合状态空间模型，并引入自动状态解释策略将拟合状态归类到代表性控制环路。
ai.result: 在跟网型和构网型换流器上验证，能准确复现动态、支持特征值分析，并揭示同步发电与跟网渗透率变化下的稳定极限。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户对黑箱换流器进行模态分析、灵敏度计算和系统级稳定性评估。

idea_spark.idea: 结合自动状态解释与降阶建模，使黑箱模型具备可解释性，便于工程应用。
idea_spark.risk: 状态解释基于频率范围近似，可能在某些复杂耦合系统中不够准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将系统辨识与物理知识结合，为黑箱模型赋予物理意义，是解决黑箱可解释性问题的有效思路。

en.tldr: A unified multi-variable fitted state-space methodology for small-signal modeling and stability analysis of black-box voltage source converters.
en.motivation: Modern power systems increasingly rely on power electronic converters, yet many are provided as black-box models, limiting conventional small-signal analysis tools.
en.method: Frequency-domain identification, adaptive pole-expansion, and reduced-order realization are used to build fitted state-space models, with an automated state-interpretation strategy assigning fitted states to representative control-loop categories.
en.result: Validated on grid-following and grid-forming converters; accurately reproduces dynamics, supports eigenvalue analysis, and reveals stability limits under varying synchronous generation and GFL penetration.
en.help_to_user: Enables modal analysis, sensitivity computation, and system-wide stability assessment for black-box converters.

idea_spark.idea: Combining automated state interpretation with reduced-order modeling gives black-box models interpretability for engineering use.
idea_spark.risk: State interpretation based on frequency ranges may be less accurate in complex coupled systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating system identification with physical knowledge to endow black-box models with physical meaning is a promising approach to address interpretability.

papers.title: Crash Assessment via Mesh-Based Graph Neural Networks and Physics-Aware Attention
papers.summary: Full-vehicle crash simulations are computationally expensive, limiting their use in iterative design exploration. This work investigates learned hybrid surrogate models (MeshTransolver, MeshGeoTransolver, and MeshGeoFLARE) for predicting time-resolved structural deformation fields in an industrial lateral pole-impact benchmark. We evaluate whether neural surrogates can reproduce full-field crash kinematics with sufficient accuracy, spatial regularity, and structural plausibility for engineering interpretation. The proposed architectures combine local mesh message passing, geometry-aware global attention, and sparse contact-aware correction for autoregressive crash rollout.   We compare mesh-based graph neural networks, attention-based geometric models, and hybrid architectures under a common training and hyperparameter configuration. The hybrid models capture both short-range structural interactions and long-range deformation patterns, while a sparse contact-aware variant assesses the effect of dynamic proximity interactions during rollout.   On a 25-sample full-vehicle test set, the best hybrid model achieves a temporal mean root-mean-square error of 3.20 mm. While geometry-aware attention baselines are quantitatively competitive, qualitative side-view inspection shows they can introduce local spatial noise and deformation irregularities that complicate structural interpretation. In contrast, hybrid mesh-attention models provide the best balance between scalar accuracy, survival-space consistency, and physically interpretable displacement fields.   These results suggest that crash surrogate assessment should combine global error metrics with downstream safety-relevant quantities and qualitative field inspection. The proposed methodology enables fast full-field predictions while preserving essential structural information for industrial crash-engineering analysis.

ai.tldr: 提出混合网格图神经网络与物理感知注意力机制，用于预测整车碰撞中的结构变形场，在工业侧柱碰撞基准上达到3.20 mm均方根误差。
ai.motivation: 整车碰撞仿真计算成本高，限制了迭代设计探索，需要快速且物理可信的替代模型。
ai.method: 结合局部网格消息传递、几何感知全局注意力和稀疏接触感知校正，构建混合代理模型（MeshTransolver、MeshGeoTransolver、MeshGeoFLARE）进行自回归碰撞展开。
ai.result: 混合模型在25样本测试集上时间均方根误差为3.20 mm，在标量精度、生存空间一致性和物理可解释位移场之间取得最佳平衡。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速预测碰撞变形场，节省仿真时间，同时保持工程可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 将稀疏接触感知注意力与图神经网络结合，以捕捉动态邻近交互，提升长程变形预测的物理合理性。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能对训练数据分布外的碰撞场景泛化不足，且定性检查中几何注意力基线存在局部噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合全局误差指标与下游安全相关量及定性场检查，为工程评估提供更全面的代理模型验证框架。
